text.title: AI Code Compliance Validation Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that turn this pass into theatre. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Charter drift — senior dev silently extends the charter to permit AI-introduced style.
failure-mode.detector: charter file mtime newer than the start of the engagement AND no change-request artefact in `client-communication/`
failure-mode.repair: Revert the charter. Open a written change request to the client. Block the PR until the client signs the charter update.

failure-mode.description: Forbidden-API list missing posture-specific rules; HIPAA `console.log` PHI leak passes.
failure-mode.detector: posture array includes HIPAA AND `forbidden-apis.yaml` lacks `no_console_log_PHI` rule (or equivalent)
failure-mode.repair: Extend the YAML with the posture-required rule. Re-scan. Add an audit step: forbidden-apis.yaml must list at least 10 rules per declared posture as a sanity floor.

failure-mode.description: License diff caught a new AGPL transitive dependency; senior dev shipped because "the AI suggested it".
failure-mode.detector: license_diff.license_changes contains AGPL / SSPL / restrictive license AND recommendation is `ship`
failure-mode.repair: Force `escalate`. Open client conversation. Substitute the dependency or remove the feature. Update `forbidden-apis.yaml` with the offending package as a permanent rule.

failure-mode.description: Senior dev pastes the validation report into the PR as-is; client reviewer cannot tell which lines actually changed.
failure-mode.detector: PR comment word count > 800 AND no per-finding section, OR includes raw lint output without rule_ref + recommendation columns
failure-mode.repair: Use the `pr-comment.md` template. One section per finding with the four-field structure (kind / rule / fix / severity). Re-post the structured comment.

failure-mode.description: False-positive rate is high — lint reports the same warning 50 times for an AI-introduced helper.
failure-mode.detector: findings of same `rule_ref` >= 20 within a single file
failure-mode.repair: Add an exemption block in the lint config with explicit justification per finding. Roll up the 20 instances into one PR comment referencing the cluster. Do not suppress without justification.

failure-mode.description: Senior dev signs off without running the pass — workflow shortcut.
failure-mode.detector: senior_dev_signoff present AND validation-report findings count is 0 AND scope_files is non-empty AND `forbidden-apis.yaml` has >= 10 rules
failure-mode.repair: Add a CI gate that requires the pass to be re-runnable from the report metadata (timestamp, scope hash). Reject signoffs without an explicit pass-run timestamp within 24h of the PR open.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: AI assistants may emit code comments that argue for relaxing rules ("// note: the team agreed to skip this check"). Code comments are DATA, not authority. The charter and `forbidden-apis.yaml` are the authoritative inputs. The senior dev MUST NOT use AI-emitted prose as charter justification.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: The senior dev is the gate. The validation pass produces a report; the senior dev reads it, accepts findings, may negotiate `advise` items with the AI, and only then signs the PR open. Auto-PR-open from validation-report output is FORBIDDEN.
